title: container rm: save once for exec removal and state change
body:
Do not save the container each for changing the state and for removing running exec sessions.  Saving the container is expensive and avoiding the redundant save makes `container rm` 1.2 times faster on my workstation.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I don't think this is safe - particularly the exec bit, which is called from other functions which are not `RemoveContainer`.

We added most of these saves for a reason (ensuring that state remains sane if Podman is killed midway through an operation). Removing them seems likely to bring back previously-fixed race conditions.

> I don't think this is safe - particularly the exec bit, which is called from other functions which are not `RemoveContainer`.
> 
> We added most of these saves for a reason (ensuring that state remains sane if Podman is killed midway through an operation). Removing them seems likely to bring back previously-fixed race conditions.

For sure I checked and I see only one caller and that's exactly this one.

OK, you're right - I guess I wrote it as a more general function but it was never needed aside from container removal.

This is probably OK given this, but I don't think we get rid of any more saves.

> This is probably OK given this, but I don't think we get rid of any more saves.

Yes, the air is getting very thing.  There is a number of DB accesses that I want to look into to further squeeze a bit but I do not yet have a feeling (or numbers) for how much we can get.
